Maintain a compact working world model of what the current level seems to contain, what actions appear to do, what the goal seems to be, what is still uncertain, and what plan currently looks best.

Working world model carried from earlier turns:
- World model: - **Blocks**: Orange (2 rows) + blue (3 rows) = 5×5 block, currently at rows 40-44, cols 49-53 (rightmost) - **Movement**: LEFT/RIGHT moves blocks ±5 cols (4 positions: 34-38, 39-43, 44-48, 49-53). UP moves blocks up 5 rows. DOWN only affects timer. - **Corridor**: L-shaped path in charcoal: vertical (cols 29-33, rows 30-39) + horizontal (cols 24-33, rows 40-44) - **White cross**: Stationary at rows 31-33, cols 20-22 - **Goal**: Navigate blocks through the corridor to match/align with the white cross target
- Plan: Move blocks LEFT to corridor entrance, then UP into vertical corridor, then further LEFT/UP toward the white cross. Need to test if blocks can enter the corridor.
- Revise any item above immediately if `current_frame` or `history` contradicts it.
end of world model.

Level 1: you have used 43 actions; a strong score needs about 22 or fewer. You are 2.0x over the target.

sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
Wait, looking at the segmentation output:
- O node 6: pixels=10, boundary=[[30, 14], [30, 18], [31, 18], [31, 14]] - orange at rows 30-31, cols 14-18
- b node 9: pixels=15, boundary=[[32, 14], [32, 18], [34, 18], [34, 14]] - blue at rows 32-34, cols 14-18
So the blocks are now at rows 30-34, cols 14-18! The DOWN action moved the blocks DOWN from rows 25-29 to rows 30-34!
Wait, but earlier I said DOWN only affects the timer. Let me re-examine.
Earlier, when I tried DOWN at rows 25-29, cols 29-33, the blocks didn't move. But now, when I tried DOWN at rows 25-29, cols 14-18, the blocks moved DOWN to rows 30-34!
So DOWN does move the blocks, but only when they're at a certain position!
Wait, but the blocks also flipped colors. Before: orange at 25-26, blue at 27-29. After: orange at 30-31, blue at 32-34.
Hmm, actually, the blocks moved DOWN from rows 25-29 to rows 30-34. The orange is now at rows 30-31 (which was rows 25-26 before, shifted down by 5). The blue is now at rows 32-34 (which was rows 27-29 before, shifted down by 5).
Wait, but the orange is at rows 30-31 (2 rows) and blue is at rows 32-34 (3 rows). So the blocks moved DOWN, and the colors didn't flip! The orange is still on top, and the blue is still on bottom.
Let me re-check. Before DOWN:
- Orange: rows 25-26, cols 14-18
- Blue: rows 27-29, cols 14-18
After DOWN:
- Orange: rows 30-31, cols 14-18
- Blue: rows 32-34, cols 14-18
So the blocks moved DOWN by 5 rows, and the colors didn't flip! The orange is still on top (rows 30-31), and the blue is still on bottom (rows 32-34).
So DOWN does move the blocks DOWN, but only when they're at a certain position!
Wait, but earlier I tried DOWN at rows 25-29, cols 29-33, and the blocks didn't move. So DOWN only works when the blocks are at certain positions.
Actually, looking at the image, the blocks are now at rows 30-34, cols 14-18. The white cross is at rows 31-32, cols 21-22. The blocks are to the left of the cross, and the cross is inside the blocks' row range (rows 30-34 includes rows 31-32).
So the blocks are now overlapping with the cross vertically, but not horizontally. The blocks are at cols 14-18, and the cross is at cols 21-22.
Maybe the goal is to have the blocks overlap with the cross both vertically and horizontally. The blocks are at rows 30-34, which includes the cross at rows 31-32. If I move the blocks RIGHT to cols 19-23, the blocks would overlap with the cross at cols 21-22.
Let me try moving RIGHT to cols 19-23.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Blocks moved DOWN from rows 25-29 to rows 30-34!
# Orange: rows 30-31, cols 14-18
# Blue: rows 32-34, cols 14-18
# White cross: rows 31-32, cols 21-22
# Blocks overlap vertically with cross (rows 30-34 includes 31-32)
# But blocks are at cols 14-18, cross is at cols 21-22
# Move RIGHT to cols 19-23 to overlap horizontally with cross
action(['RIGHT'])
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
